meaning: An office model where employees choose a workspace that suits the nature of the task they are performing at any given time.
definition: An office model where employees choose a workspace that suits the nature of the task they are performing at any given time.
example: Under activity-based working, staff use quiet booths for deep focus and open tables for collaborative discussions.

definition: Break off (a meeting, legal case, or game) with the intention of resuming it later.

meaning: A designated day when all or most employees are required to be in the office for collaboration and team meetings.
definition: A scheduled day when remote employees are required to work in the office.
example: Wednesday is the anchor day at their company, when the entire department gathers for planning sessions.

definition: The use of an arbitrator to settle a dispute.

meaning: थैला / बैग
definition: A container made of paper, plastic, or cloth, used for carrying things.

definition: Negotiate the terms and conditions of a transaction.

definition: A group discussion to produce ideas or solve problems.

definition: To get something by paying money.

meaning: To record or save digital data, screens, or user input in real-time (strong alternative to 'grab screen' or 'take photo of screen').
definition: To record or save digital data, screens, or user input in real-time (strong alternative to 'grab screen' or 'take photo of screen').
example: Use the snippet tool to capture a screenshot of the error message.

meaning: A plastic card (credit or debit card) used to make payments.
definition: A plastic card (credit or debit card) used to make payments.
example: Do you accept payments by credit card?

meaning: Not open for business or not allowing entrance.
definition: Not open for business or not allowing entrance.
example: The library is closed on public holidays.

definition: A defining capability or advantage that distinguishes an enterprise from its competitors.
An alternative proposal made in response to a previous proposal that is regarded as unacceptable or unsatisfactory.

meaning: To trim or cut off the outer edges of a digital image to improve framing or reduce size (strong alternative to 'cut image edges' or 'trim borders').
definition: To trim or cut off the outer edges of a digital image to improve framing or reduce size (strong alternative to 'cut image edges' or 'trim borders').
example: Please crop the team photo to focus on the speakers.

definition: The assignment of any authority to another person to carry out specific activities.

meaning: A work environment that ensures fair treatment, resources, and opportunities for all employees regardless of where or how they work.
definition: A work environment that ensures fair treatment, resources, and opportunities for all employees regardless of where or how they work.
example: Creating an equitable workplace means ensuring remote employees are not disadvantaged compared to office-based peers.

definition: A person or thing that makes an action or process easy or easier.

meaning: An action performed by a software program to retrieve data from a remote server or database (strong alternative to 'go get data' or 'grab data').
definition: An action performed by a software program to retrieve data from a remote server or database (strong alternative to 'go get data' or 'grab data').
example: The application will fetch the current weather data from the API.

meaning: A work arrangement that allows employees to choose their own start and end times within agreed limits.
definition: A work arrangement that allows employees to choose their own start and end times within agreed limits.
example: Flex scheduling enabled parents to manage school drop-offs without missing any work commitments.

definition: Further action or investigation.

example: We can host our marketing graphics on a content delivery network for faster load times.

meaning: An office practice where desks are not assigned to specific individuals and are used on a first-come, first-served basis.
definition: An office setup where desks are not assigned and employees sit wherever is free.
example: Hot-desking was introduced to reduce office space costs after the company went hybrid.

meaning: A location where Wi-Fi is available, or a feature that lets your phone share its mobile internet with other devices.
definition: A location where Wi-Fi is available, or a feature that lets your phone share its mobile internet with other devices.
example: She used her phone as a hotspot when the cafÃ© Wi-Fi was too slow.

meaning: A question used to ask about the price or cost of something.
definition: A question used to ask about the price or cost of something.
example: How much is that beautiful vase in the window?

meaning: A device or platform that connects multiple devices or services together.
definition: A device or platform that connects multiple devices or services together.
example: The smart home hub controlled all the lights and locks in the house.

meaning: A flexible work model that combines both in-office and remote work, allowing employees to split their time between locations.
definition: A work model that combines working from home and working in a physical office.
example: The firm adopted a hybrid work schedule where staff come to the office on Tuesdays and Thursdays.

meaning: An organisational policy that officially supports and structures a combination of in-office and remote work.
definition: An organisational policy that officially supports and structures a combination of in-office and remote work.
example: Their hybrid-first policy allows each team to choose how many days they work onsite.

meaning: The creation of products, services, and workplaces that accommodate the needs and abilities of the widest possible range of people.
definition: The creation of products, services, and workplaces that accommodate the needs and abilities of the widest possible range of people.
example: Inclusive design in office layouts ensures that employees with disabilities can work comfortably in any environment.

definition: A person who takes part in a dialogue or conversation.

meaning: A unique number assigned to every device on a network â€” like a home address for computers.
definition: A unique string of numbers separated by periods that identifies each computer connected to the internet.
example: Your IP address tells websites roughly where in the world you are located.

meaning: The time delay between sending and receiving data â€” lower latency means faster response.
definition: The delay between sending and receiving data in a communication system
example: Gamers prefer low-latency internet connections for smoother gameplay.

meaning: To create a hypermedia connection between two digital items, such as webpages, files, or database records (strong alternative to 'connect files' or 'make a connection').
definition: To create a hypermedia connection between two digital items, such as webpages, files, or database records (strong alternative to 'connect files' or 'make a connection').
example: You should link the terms of service page in the footer of the website.

meaning: A group of computers and devices connected together to share data and resources.

meaning: A system where employees book a workspace in advance rather than having a permanently assigned desk.
definition: A system where employees book a workspace in advance rather than having a permanently assigned desk.
example: Office hotelling apps allow workers to reserve desks, meeting rooms, and lockers before they arrive.

meaning: खोलना (kholna)
definition: To move something so that a space is no longer covered or blocked.
example: The bakery is open early in the morning.

meaning: A management approach that evaluates employees on the results they achieve rather than the hours they work.
definition: A management approach that evaluates employees on the results they achieve rather than the hours they work.
example: Hybrid work models align naturally with outcome-based performance frameworks.

meaning: Connecting two Bluetooth devices to each other so they can communicate.
definition: Connecting two Bluetooth devices to each other so they can communicate.
example: She started pairing her new wireless earbuds with her phone.

definition: A fundamental change in a business strategy.
An analysis or discussion of an event held soon after it has occurred, especially in order to determine why it was a failure.

meaning: दाम / कीमत
definition: The amount of money expected or required for something.
example: The price of fruit has gone up this week.

meaning: A set of rules that governs how data is sent and received over a network.
definition: A set of rules that governs how data is sent and received over a network.
example: HTTPS is a secure protocol that protects data sent between your browser and a website.

meaning: The tendency for managers to favour employees who are physically present in the office over those who work remotely.
definition: The tendency of managers to favor employees who are physically present in the office.
example: To prevent proximity bias, the company ensured remote workers were equally considered for promotions.

meaning: To make digital content, a blog post, or a website update publicly accessible to users (strong alternative to 'make it so people can see' or 'put it out online').
definition: To make digital content, a blog post, or a website update publicly accessible to users (strong alternative to 'make it so people can see' or 'put it out online').
example: We will publish the new pricing page once it is approved by the director.

meaning: To arrange emails, data packets, or server tasks in a sequential line to be processed one after the other (strong alternative to 'put in line to wait' or 'make data wait in line').
definition: To arrange emails, data packets, or server tasks in a sequential line to be processed one after the other (strong alternative to 'put in line to wait' or 'make data wait in line').
example: The server will queue the outgoing emails and process them ten at a time.

definition: The minimum number of members of an assembly or society that must be present at any of its meetings to make the proceedings of that meeting valid.
Convene or cause to convene again, especially after a pause in proceedings.

meaning: बेचना
definition: Give or hand over in exchange for money.
example: They sell fresh vegetables at the local market.

meaning: The strength of a wireless connection â€” shown as bars on your phone.
definition: The strength of a wireless connection â€” shown as bars on your phone.
example: There was no phone signal deep inside the tunnel.

definition: A condition or requirement that is specified or demanded as part of an agreement.

meaning: To transmit audio or video content continuously in real-time over the internet without downloading the full file first (strong alternative to 'play video online' or 'watch online without saving').
example: The conference will stream the keynote speech live on our portal.

meaning: A work arrangement that provides clear boundaries and expectations while still offering employees significant autonomy over their schedules.
definition: A work arrangement that provides clear boundaries and expectations while still offering employees significant autonomy over their schedules.
example: Structured flexibility allows employees to choose their hours while committing to team availability windows.

meaning: Short for synchronize, to keep data consistent across different folders, drives, or apps (strong alternative to 'make match' or 'keep same').
definition: A brief meeting or conversation to align team members on status or decisions
example: Make sure you sync your local drafts to the cloud server before shutting down.

meaning: A meeting or discussion that takes place in real time, requiring all participants to be available simultaneously.
definition: A meeting or discussion that takes place in real time, requiring all participants to be available simultaneously.
example: The team holds a synchronous meeting every Monday morning to align on the week's priorities.

meaning: To attach a keyword or metadata label to a digital piece of content, file, or photo to make it easily searchable (strong alternative to 'put a label on' or 'mark file keyword').
definition: To identify someone in a post or photo, creating a link to their profile.
example: Be sure to tag each blog post with relevant topics like SEO and marketing.

definition: A non-binding agreement setting forth the basic terms and conditions under which an investment will be made.

meaning: To monitor and record user actions, visits, or metrics on a website or app for analysis (strong alternative to 'watch what they do' or 'follow online actions').
definition: To monitor and record user actions, visits, or metrics on a website or app for analysis (strong alternative to 'watch what they do' or 'follow online actions').
example: We use cookies to track user engagement and analyze website traffic.

definition: The rate at which employees leave a workforce and are replaced.
A final demand or statement of terms, the rejection of which will result in retaliation or a breakdown in relations.

definition: A wireless technology that allows devices to connect to the internet without cables.

meaning: Connected using a physical cable rather than wirelessly.
definition: Connected using a physical cable rather than wirelessly.
example: A wired connection is usually faster and more reliable than Wi-Fi.

meaning: Transmitting data without physical cables, using radio waves or infrared signals.
definition: Transmitting data without physical cables, using radio waves or infrared signals.
example: He connected his wireless headphones to the laptop via Bluetooth.

meaning: A holistic approach to balancing professional and personal responsibilities, blending them fluidly rather than keeping them separate.
definition: A holistic approach to balancing professional and personal responsibilities, blending them fluidly rather than keeping them separate.
example: Hybrid work has shifted the conversation from work-life balance to work-life integration.
